english: ment; _ots'em-skúd ('tshem skud) thread for sewing; _ots'em-k'áb ('tshem khab) needle. -- _ots'em-drúb ('tshem drub) needle-work Cs. -- _ots'em-srúb ('tshem srub) W. seam. -- _ots'em-méd ('tshem med) without a seam; Sch. also: without interruption.

I. vb. to neigh Pth. and vulgo. — II. also mts'ér-ba (mtsher ba) 1. vb. to grieve, to sorrow, and sbst. grief, sorrow, resp. t'ugs-ts'ér (thugs 'tsher), cf. ts'ér-k'a (tsher kha); ts'ér-c'àn ('tsher can) sorrowful, anxious, ts'er-méd ('tsher med) free from sorrow, easy. — 2. to be afraid, to fear C., Mil. — 3. to shine, to glitter, and sbst. lustre, brightness, splendour, brilliancy, of light Lex., of jewels Dzl.; dkár-źiṅ (dkar zhing) (or dkár-la (dkar la)) ts'ér-ba ('tsher ba) to be of a shining white Mil.
1. Sch.: cause of uneasiness, source of care. — 2. an old deserted settlement or dwelling; tser-rnyiṅ (tser rnying) id. Sch.

I. vb. a. intrs, pf. and imp. sos (sos), 1. to live, riṅ-du (ring du) a long time, lo brgya (lo brgya) a hundred years Med.; nam (nam) (or ji-srid (ji srid)) _ots'öi bár-du ('tsho'i bar du) for life, life-long, c'ós-kyis (chos kyis), ríg-pas (rig pas), rṅón-pas (rngon pas) to gain a livelihood by religion, science, hunting Cs., or: to lead the life of a cleric, scholar, hunter; srid _ots'ó-ba (srid 'tsho ba) to pass life, to continue in a state, to exist, frq.; _odú-_odzii náṅ-du _ots'o mi p'ód-do ('du 'dzi'i nang du 'tsho mi phod do) in the throng of the world I cannot exist Dzl. (W. *són-c'e (son che) and tse p'íd-c'e* (tse phid che)). — 2. to remain alive, to be maintained in life, _odi ma byás-na mi _ots'oo ('di ma byas na mi 'tsho'o) else we shall not remain alive, we shall not be able to live Dzl.; to revive, to recover, from sickness etc. Dzl.; sós-par _ogyúr-ba (sos par 'gyur ba) id, frq.; śi-ba-las (shi ba las) to be rescued from peril of death Dzl. — 3. to last, to be durable, of clothes etc., W.: *mán-po ts'ó-c'e* (man po tsho che) to last long, to be very durable; _ots'ó-źiṅ sdód-pa ('tsho zhing sdod pa) to remain valid, binding, to retain its virtue, efficacy, of laws, doctrine etc. — 4. to feed, to graze. — b. trs., pf. (b)sos ((b)sos), fut. gso (gso), 1. to nourish, lus (lus) the body; to sustain, srog (srog) life; to pasture, to feed, p'yugs _ots'ó-ba-la kyér-ba (phyugs 'tsho ba la kyer ba) to lead the cattle to pasture Pth., p'yugs _ots'or p'yín-pa (phyugs 'tshor phyin pa) id. — 2. to heal, to cure, nad (nad) Lt.; in this sense the fut. form is used as a vb. for itself, q. v.; _otso-byéd ('tsho byed), tso-mdzád (tsho mdzad) 'life-giver', i. e. physician, medicine. II. sbst., also _ots'o ('tsho), 1. life, mi źíg-gi _ots'ó-ba bsól-ba (mi zhig gi 'tsho ba bsol ba) to prolong life Dzl.; _oó-c'ag _ots'öi rje ('o cag 'tsho'i rje) the lord of our lives, viz. the king Glr.; _ots'o skyóṅ-ba ('tsho skyong ba) to spare, preserve, protect another's life; to rear, bring up, educate. — 2. livelihood, sustenance, nourishment, entertainment, zlá-ba gsúm-gyi bár-du _ots'ó-ba sbyór-ba (zla ba gsum gyi bar du 'tsho ba sbyor ba) to board a person for three months Dzl.; _ots'ó-ba-la ma bltá-ste ('tsho ba la ma blta ste) not caring for the entertainment Dzl.; _ots'ó-bab záṅ-po ('tsho bab zang po) good eating and drinking Mil.
goods, effects, chattels, tools, necessaries, = yo-byad (yo byad) Lex.; also provisions, provender.

pf. btsags (btsags), fut. btsog (btsog), imp. ts'og (tshog), W. *ts'óg-c'e* (tshog che) 1. to hew, chop, cut, pierce; to inoculate, vaccinate, brúm-pa (brum pa) the small-pox. — 2. to cudgel, _ots'óg-ciṅ rdúṅ-ba ('tshog cing rdung ba) Pth., brdóg-_ots'og-pa (brdog 'tshog pa) id. Dzl. — 3. also mts'óg-pa (mtshog pa) to find fault with, to blame, censure, carp at, teaze Sch.

pf. and imp. ts'ogs (tshogs), to assemble, to gather, to meet, frq.; k'yed _odir ts'ogs (khyed 'dir tshogs), ye, that are here assembled Mil.; mi máṅ-po ts'ógs-pai mdún-du (mi mang po tshogs pa'i mdun du) before many assembled people Dzl.; _obyúṅ-ba lṅa ts'ógs-pa ('byung ba lnga tshogs pa) the five elements meeting S.g.; _otsogs rtén-gyi zas-c'áṅ ('tshogs rten gyi zas chang) food and drink to entertain the people assembled Glr.; to unite, to join in doing something, to associate, to make common cause; examples v. lugs (lugs).

pf. btsoṅs (btsongs), fut. btsoṅ (btsong), imp. tsoṅ (tsong), W. *ts'óṅ-c'e* (tshong che), to sell, dri _ots'óṅ-bai gnas (dri 'tshong ba'i gnas) place where perfumes are sold Stg.; *daṅ gón-c'e ts'óṅ-k'an-ni mi* (dang gon che tshong khan ni mi) W. the man that yesterday had a coat to sell.

(Cs. _ots'ó-ba? ('tsho ba?)) pf. btsos (btsos), fut. btso (btso), imp. tsos (tsos), tsod (tsod), W. *ts'ó-c'e* (tsho che), 1. to cook, to dress, in boiling water, meat, vegetables; *c'u-ts'ós* (chu tshos) W. 'water-boiled', dumplings, = *c'ú-ta-gir* (chu ta gir). — 2. to bake provinc. — 3. to dye, gos (gos) a garment. — 4. ts'ós-pa (tshos pa), *ts'ós-mk'an* (tshos mkhan) W.* ripe, *tsos soṅ* (tsos song) is ripe; *ldád-pa ma tsos* (ldad pa ma tsos) Ld., he is a green-horn.
